meaning: adj.反作用的,反动的n.反动分子
examples: Critics viewed him as a reactionary, even a monarchist.
The reactionary policemen broke and arrested many progressive students.
The reactionary policemen broke in and arrested many progressive students.
Like American reactionary movements of the past, the tea party resists change of any kind.
Rather than a defensive and reactionary approach to these legitimate business concerns, CSR is proactive.
